pattern: Every solid block of ones forms its own rectangle, so scanning for a cell that is a rectangle's top-left corner - no filled neighbor directly above or to the left - and then walking right and down from there recovers the whole block in one pass.
hints: Every block of filled cells is guaranteed to form a solid rectangle, never an irregular shape.
A cell only needs to be treated as the start of a new block when the cell above it and the cell to its left are both empty (or off the grid).

From a detected top-left cell, extend straight down while the column stays filled to find the bottom row, and extend straight right while the row stays filled to find the last column; that pins down the whole rectangle in one pass, order of the rectangles found does not matter.

edge_inputs.description: the whole grid is a single block
single empty cell, no blocks at all
two single-cell blocks touching only at a corner, not an edge
several differently shaped blocks separated by empty buffer rows and columns
no farmland anywhere in a larger grid

stress.description: grid built from a layout of block rows and columns, each block a solid rectangle or empty, always separated by an empty buffer so blocks never merge
stress.note: buffer rows/cols between every block guarantee the rectangle invariant the problem promises
